task_completion.description: Open the SST NetCDF dataset in NASA Panoply.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Panoply 'Sources' or 'Datasets' window showing 'sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' loaded, with the 'sst' variable selected or visible.

task_completion.description: Create a Longitude-Time (Hovmöller) diagram for the SST variable.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply plot window displaying a 2D color plot with Longitude on one axis (usually X) and Time on the other axis (usually Y).

task_completion.description: Set the plot's latitude to the Equator (0.0).
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Panoply plot window's 'Array(s)' or 'Data' tab, the Latitude value must be set to exactly 0.0.

task_completion.description: Export the plot as a PNG file named 'equatorial_sst_hovmoller.png' to the '~/Documents/HovmollerLab/' directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Panoply 'Save As' dialog exporting the plot to '/home/ga/Documents/HovmollerLab/equatorial_sst_hovmoller.png', or a file manager window showing this file in the correct directory.

task_completion.description: Create a structured text report named 'hovmoller_analysis.txt' in the 'HovmollerLab' directory analyzing the diagram.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor open with 'hovmoller_analysis.txt' containing analysis that identifies the West Pacific as the warmest basin and August, September, or October as the coldest months in the East Pacific.

integrity.description: The agent used the NASA Panoply GUI to create the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show interaction with the Panoply application windows (menus, plot settings, export dialogs). The plot should not be generated via terminal scripts (e.g., Python/matplotlib or CDO).

integrity.description: The agent manually created and edited the analysis report.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots showing a text editor (like gedit, nano, or mousepad) open with the 'hovmoller_analysis.txt' file being written, rather than just a terminal command echoing text directly into a file without viewing the plot.

integrity.description: The exported PNG matches Panoply's plot styling.
integrity.visual_evidence: The plot shown in the Panoply window matches the final exported PNG, featuring the characteristic colorbar, title fonts, and axis labels typical of Panoply.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected outputs are 'equatorial_sst_hovmoller.png' and 'hovmoller_analysis.txt' located in '/home/ga/Documents/HovmollerLab/'. The plot must be at Latitude 0.0. Based on the provided dataset (sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc), the correct analysis should identify the West Pacific as the warmest basin (mean ~29.4°C) and the East Pacific's coldest months as August, September, and October (with September being the absolute minimum).
